# @zakkster/lite-headless/switch

Headless boolean toggle control with WAI-ARIA `role="switch"`
semantics. Visually a sliding switch (consumer-styled); semantically
distinct from a checkbox -- a switch is for INSTANT-COMMIT settings
("Enable notifications", "Dark mode"), a checkbox is for selection
state that's later submitted via a form.

WAI-ARIA APG: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/switch/

## Quick example

```js
import { createSwitch } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/switch";

const sw = createSwitch({
    defaultChecked: false,
    onChange: (checked, reason) => {
        console.log("now:", checked, "via:", reason);
    },
});

sw.attachRoot (rootEl);    // role=switch, listeners, ARIA
sw.attachLabel(labelEl);   // aria-labelledby auto-wired
sw.attachThumb(thumbEl);   // visual thumb (gets data-checked)
sw.attachInput(inputEl);   // optional native checkbox for forms
```

## Options

| Option           | Default | Notes                                              |
| ---------------- | ------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| `defaultChecked` | `false` | Uncontrolled seed                                  |
| `checked`        | -       | Signal<boolean> for controlled mode (read-only)    |
| `disabled`       | `false` | Initial disabled state                             |
| `required`       | `false` | Sets aria-required + on native input if attached   |
| `onChange`       | -       | `(checked, reason) => void`                        |

`reason` values: `"click"`, `"keyboard"`, `"label-click"`,
`"input-change"`, `"toggle"`, `"set"`, plus any custom string
passed to `setChecked(v, "custom-reason")`.

## Controlled vs uncontrolled

```js
// Uncontrolled (default): primitive owns the state
const sw = createSwitch({ defaultChecked: true });
sw.toggle();                  // primitive flips its internal signal

// Controlled: external signal owns the state
const myState = signal(false);
const sw = createSwitch({
    checked: myState,
    onChange: (v) => myState.set(v),    // consumer updates manually
});
```

In controlled mode the primitive READS from your signal but never
writes to it. The consumer is responsible for updating in response
to `onChange`. This is the same contract as React's controlled
inputs.

## Attachment methods

| Method              | Effect                                                                |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `attachRoot(el)`    | role=switch, aria-checked, tabindex, click/keyboard listeners        |
| `attachLabel(el)`   | aria-labelledby auto-wired; click on label toggles (and focuses root)|
| `attachThumb(el)`   | visual element; gets `data-checked` and `data-disabled` synced        |
| `attachInput(el)`   | hidden native checkbox for `<form>` submission                        |

The root is typically a `<button type="button">` for native keyboard
semantics, but the primitive doesn't enforce -- any focusable element
works.

## CSS contract

The root and thumb both get these attributes synced to state:

```css
[data-switch-root] {
    /* default styles: off-state track */
}
[data-switch-root][data-checked="true"] {
    /* on-state track */
}
[data-switch-root][data-disabled] {
    /* dimmed appearance */
}
[data-switch-root][data-pressed] {
    /* active-press feedback */
}
[data-switch-thumb] {
    /* default thumb position */
}
[data-switch-thumb][data-checked="true"] {
    /* shifted thumb position */
}
```

## Keyboard

| Key             | Action          |
| --------------- | --------------- |
| Space           | Toggle          |
| Enter           | Toggle (also)   |

Per WAI-ARIA APG, Space is the primary toggle key. Enter is
"optional" but widely-expected, so the primitive supports both.

## Form integration

If you want native `<form>` submission, pass a hidden native
`<input type="checkbox">` to `attachInput(el)`. The primitive
syncs state in both directions:

- Primitive state -> input.checked (so form submission gets the value)
- Input.change (e.g., from browser autofill) -> primitive state

The native input also serves as the no-JS fallback. Style it
visually-hidden so the consumer's switch markup is what the user sees:

```css
input[type="checkbox"].lh-switch-input {
    position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px;
    clip: rect(0,0,0,0); border: 0; padding: 0; margin: -1px;
    overflow: hidden; white-space: nowrap;
}
```

The custom element wrapper does this automatically when `name=` is set.

## Custom element wrapper

```html
<lite-switch name="notifications" default-checked>
    <span data-switch-label>Notifications</span>
    <button data-switch-root type="button" aria-label="Notifications">
        <span data-switch-thumb></span>
    </button>
</lite-switch>
```

### Attributes

| Attribute         | Effect                                                       |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `default-checked` | Initial checked state (uncontrolled)                         |
| `checked`         | Reactive: set/remove to update state from outside            |
| `disabled`        | Reactive: set/remove to toggle disabled                      |
| `required`        | Sets aria-required + native input required                   |
| `name`            | Creates auto-hidden checkbox for form submission             |
| `value`           | Value attr on the hidden input (default `"on"`)              |

### Imperative API on host

```js
host.toggle(reason?)
host.setChecked(bool, reason?)
host.setDisabled(bool)
host.checked   // read-only
host.disabled  // read-only
```

### Dispatched events

- `change` -- `{ detail: { checked, reason } }`

## Comparison to checkbox / radio

| Use switch for...           | Use checkbox/radio for...                |
| --------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| Instant-commit settings     | Form values submitted on submit          |
| "On/Off" semantic           | "Selected/Unselected" semantic           |
| Dark mode, notifications,   | Agree to terms, select multiple options, |
| feature flags, sync, etc.   | radio buttons in a group                 |

The visual difference is also semantic: users intuit that flipping
a switch immediately changes a system state, while toggling a
checkbox typically defers the change until submission.
